clusters.label: A
clusters.summary: The San Bernardino Mountains are experiencing extreme fire conditions with 152 hexagons at VERY_HIGH risk, driven by geographic location, satellite daytime surface temperature (44.8°C), and 7-day average daytime surface temperature (44.8°C). Temperatures are reaching 104.2°F, and wind gusts are up to 49.6mph, creating a high risk of rapid fire spread.
actions: Deploy Type 1 engines within 15-min response time of high-risk areas.
Position aerial resources on standby at nearby air bases.
Activate CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocol to high-risk residents, advising them to prepare defensible space and go-bags.

clusters.label: B
clusters.summary: The Shasta-Trinity Region is experiencing elevated fire conditions with 3 hexagons at HIGH risk, driven by peak fire season timing, satellite daytime surface temperature (45.9°C), and geographic location. Temperatures are reaching 102.9°F, and drought conditions are severe.
actions: Increase aerial resource presence in the area, with a focus on water bombing and retardant deployment.
Position Type 1 engines in strategic locations to respond quickly to emerging fires.
Activate CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocol to high-risk residents, advising them to prepare defensible space and go-bags.

clusters.label: C
clusters.summary: The Shasta-Trinity Region is experiencing elevated fire conditions with 3 hexagons at HIGH risk, driven by peak fire season timing, satellite daytime surface temperature (42.7°C), and geographic location. Temperatures are reaching 103.2°F, and drought conditions are severe.
actions: Increase aerial resource presence in the area, with a focus on water bombing and retardant deployment.
Position Type 1 engines in strategic locations to respond quickly to emerging fires.
Activate CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocol to high-risk residents, advising them to prepare defensible space and go-bags.

clusters.label: D
clusters.summary: The Shasta-Trinity Region is experiencing elevated fire conditions with 4 hexagons at HIGH risk, driven by geographic location, 7-day average daytime surface temperature (47.2°C), and 30-day average precipitation. Temperatures are reaching 101.0°F, and drought conditions are moderate.
actions: Position Type 1 engines in strategic locations to respond quickly to emerging fires.
Activate CAL FIRE READY-SET-GO protocol to high-risk residents, advising them to prepare defensible space and go-bags.
Increase water tender presence in the area, with a focus on rapid fire suppression.

overall_assessment: California is experiencing a significant increase in fire risk, with 143 hexagons at VERY_HIGH risk and 27 hexagons at HIGH risk. The San Bernardino Mountains are the most critical area, with extreme fire conditions and a high risk of rapid fire spread. CAL FIRE is activating READY-SET-GO protocols and deploying resources to high-risk areas to mitigate the risk of wildfires.
